{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = X1:T13-D2-t:t-T13-W12-D21:t-N14 N33:N33-N33:N33-N33:N33:N33Die Verwendung der Hieroglyphe T13 statt T14 widerspricht der üblichen Schreibung. Ursache ist die teilweise [[Altägyptische Kryptographie|kryptographische Schreibung]] des Nutbuches, vgl. Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, S. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat heret Temat cheret
Lesung aber: Temat heret cheret
''{{unicode|tm3.t ḥr.t ẖr.t}}'' }} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' war die [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] Bezeichnung eines [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der als [[Baktiu]] insgesamt sieben [[Stern]]e umfasste. == Hintergrund == Im [[Nutbuch]] wird Temat-heret-cheret in den [[Dekanlisten (Nutbuch)|Dekanlisten]] an verschiedenen Positionen geführt. In den Särgen der Epoche [[Mentuhotep II.]] repräsentierte Temat-heret-cheret als führendes Baktiu-Sternbild die erste [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekade]]. [[Sesostris III.]] veranlasste in seinem siebten Regierungsjahr die Neuordnung der Dekanliste, in der Temat-heret-cheret nunmehr auf Position fünf geführt wurde. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Schemu|16. Schemu II]] datiert. Die führende Rolle des ersten Baktiu übernahm [[Kenmut]], der zu jener Zeit am 6. Schemu I seinen heliakischen Aufgang hatte. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne]] == Literatur == * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Astronomie}} [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Astronomie]] [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Mythologie]]

[[Sesostris III.]] veranlasste in seinem siebten Regierungsjahr die Neuordnung der Dekanliste, in der Temat-heret-cheret nunmehr auf Position fünf geführt wurde. Juli (das Sichtungsdatum ist vom Bezugsort abhängig). seinen heliakischen Aufgang hatte. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne]] == Literatur == * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Astronomie}} [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Astronomie]] [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Mythologie]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33Die Verwendung der Hieroglyphe T13 statt T14 widerspricht der üblichen Schreibung. Im Originaltext ist T13 zusätzlich gespiegelt und um 90° gedreht zu sehen. Ursache ist die teilweise [[Altägyptische Kryptographie|kryptographische Schreibung]] des Nutbuches, vgl. Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, S. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''{{unicode|Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t}}''
Lesung: Temat-heret-cheret
''{{unicode|Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t}}''
''Oberer und Unterer der Matte''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der sieben [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Dieser Dekan war in die zwei untergeordneten Einzeldekane Temat-heret und Temat-cheret geteilt. == Hintergrund == [[Bild:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Kreuz des Südens]] Die auffälligsten [[Himmelsobjekt]]e sind hierbei die vier [[Stern|Hauptsterne]] [[Acrux]], [[Becrux]], [[Gacrux]] und [[Decrux]] vom [[Sternbild]] [[Kreuz des Südens]]. Diese vier Hauptsterne bilden eine gut erkennbare [[Raute]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Temat-heret-cheret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den fünfter Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|16. Schemu II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 85–86. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. == Einzelnachweise == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33Die Verwendung der Hieroglyphe T13 statt T14 widerspricht der üblichen Schreibung. Im Originaltext ist T13 zusätzlich gespiegelt und um 90° gedreht zu sehen. Ursache ist die teilweise [[Altägyptische Kryptographie|kryptographische Schreibung]] des Nutbuches, vgl. Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, S. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''{{unicode|Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t}}''
Lesung: Temat-heret-cheret
''{{unicode|Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t}}''
''Oberer und Unterer der Matte''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' ist zugleich die Bezeichnung des [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Sternbild]]es '''Matte''' sowie eines altägyptischen [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der sieben [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und die zwei Einzeldekane [[Temat-heret]] und [[Temat-cheret]] beinhaltete. In den [[Dekanlisten A1]] stand Temat-heret-cheret an erster Position. == Hintergrund == [[Bild:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Kreuz des Südens]] Die auffälligsten [[Himmelsobjekt]]e sind hierbei die vier [[Stern|Hauptsterne]] [[Acrux]], [[Becrux]], [[Gacrux]] und [[Decrux]] vom [[Sternbild]] [[Kreuz des Südens]]. Diese vier Hauptsterne bilden eine gut erkennbare [[Raute]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Temat-heret-cheret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den fünften Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|16. Schemu II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 85–86. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. == Einzelnachweise == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Sternbild in der altägyptischen Astronomie]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = <hiero>t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33</hiero><ref>The use of the hieroglyph <hiero>T13</hiero> instead of <hiero>T14</hiero> contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, <hiero>T13</hiero> is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Lau..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|heliacal|de|Heliakisch}} rising was scheduled for the {{ill|Shemu|de|Schemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''

These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|heliacal|de|Heliakisch}} rising was scheduled for the {{ill|Shemu|de|Schemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]][[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

The {{ill|heliacal|de|Heliakisch}} rising was scheduled for the {{ill|Shemu|de|Schemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]][[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Shemu|de|Schemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Temat-heret-cheret-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''

These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qqqqqq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:qqqqqq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:emat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqq]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:emat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:emat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:emat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:emat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:New]]

''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] ''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=draft}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=draft}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars|12=draft}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux|12=draft}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux|12=draft}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens|12=draft}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=draft}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu|12=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu|12=draft|12=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]]

The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|lt=Shemu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|lt=Shemu|12=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars|qq=draft}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens|qq=draft}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|lt=Shemu|qq=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|lt=Shemu|qq=draft}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian|qq=}} {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/constellation|qq=}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan|qq=}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main {{ill|star|de|Stern|lt=stars|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/star|qq=}} {{ill|Acrux|de|Acrux|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acrux|qq=}}, {{ill|Becrux|de|Becrux|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becrux|qq=}}, {{ill|Gacrux|de|Gacrux|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gacrux|qq=}}, and {{ill|Decrux|de|Decrux|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decrux|qq=}} of the {{ill|constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/constellation|qq=}} {{ill|Southern Cross|de|Kreuz des Südens|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Cross|qq=}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|lt=Shemu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|qq=}} ({{ill|12th Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/12th Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]]

These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}.
