revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. ''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''

These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellation in ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[en:draft:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]]

Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:qq]]

''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]]}} [[sn:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[sn:emat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}}

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian [[:en:Decan|Decan]] consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main [[:en:Star|stars]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]], [[:en:Mimosa (star)|Becrux]], [[:en:Gacrux|Gacrux]], and [[:en:Delta Crucis|Decrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|constellation]] [[:en:Crux|Southern Cross]]. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was scheduled for the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Shemu]] 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]" to "[[Category:qq]]"
revision.text:
[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2402471]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
