revision.text:
[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527|1=Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466|1=Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux|1=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux|1=Mimosa (star)}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux|1=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux|1=Delta Crucis}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross|1=Crux}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527|1=Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466|1=Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux|1=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux|1=Mimosa (star)}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux|1=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux|1=Delta Crucis}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross|1=Crux}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・ヘレト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402471}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] [[de:Temat-heret-cheret]] {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527|1=Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466|1=Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux|1=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux|1=Mimosa (star)}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux|1=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux|1=Delta Crucis}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross|1=Crux}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyph |NAME = t:T13-D2:t t:T13-W12-D21:t N14:N14-N33*N33*N33:N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 contradicts the usual writing style. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated by 90°. The reason is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the Book of Nut; see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-heret Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t tm3.t-ẖr.t''
Reading: Temat-heret-cheret
''Tm3.t-ḥr.t-ẖr.t''
''Upper and Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-heret-cheret''' is both the name of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} "Mat" and an ancient Egyptian {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}} consisting of seven {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} stars and including the two individual decans {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527|1=Temat-heret}} and {{ill|Temat-cheret|de|Temat-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402466|1=Temat-cheret}}. == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most notable {{ill|celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}}s here are the four main {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux|1=Acrux}}, {{ill|Mimosa (star)|en|Mimosa (star)|WD=Q13105|lt=Becrux|1=Mimosa (star)}}, {{ill|Gacrux|en|Gacrux|WD=Q14233|lt=Gacrux|1=Gacrux}}, and {{ill|Delta Crucis|en|Delta Crucis|WD=Q333243|lt=Decrux|1=Delta Crucis}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Crux|en|Crux|WD=Q10542|lt=Southern Cross|1=Crux}}. These four main stars form a well-recognizable diamond shape. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|lt=Book of Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic Period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text), Temat-heret-cheret represented the fifth decan on the body of the {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was scheduled for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Shemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} 16th II and was based on the ordered arrangement under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Sesostris III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh regnal year. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-heret-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{wikidata link|Q2402471|de|Temat-heret-cheret|ja|テマト・ヘレト・チェレト}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Constellations in Ancient Egyptian Astronomy]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
